page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 192 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is supported by 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance, as measured by stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression, falls into the lower 25th percentile. However, multi-core execution, including stress-ng multi-core, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core tests, consistently registers in the average range. Memory bandwidth and database operations also perform at average levels, with Redis SET operations reaching 37.85 million operations per second.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the m5dn.12xlarge is defined by its high-speed 50 Gbps networking and integrated 1800 GB NVMe local storage, which reduce the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput workloads. However, the tradeoff lies in its moderate compute density; while multi-core scaling is stable, workloads requiring high single-thread performance or advanced AI inference may find the Intel Xeon 8259CL architecture limiting, as evidenced by weak performance in large-scale LLM text generation.

This instance is suited for workloads requiring a balance of compute, memory, and high-speed local storage. Appropriate use cases include medium-sized databases, distributed file systems, caching layers like Redis, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that benefit from the 50 Gbps network baseline.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 192 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core execution and single-threaded compression fall into the lower tier. The instance offers a balanced resource profile, making it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring high network throughput and local storage without needing top-tier single-thread compute. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform featuring high-speed local NVMe storage and 50 Gbps networking for data-intensive multi-threaded workloads.

1800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications
Average multi-core and database benchmark performance
Suited for databases, caching layers, and static web serving
